summary: PR 2520 linked the item translation unit melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c by adding its ItemStateTable data definition and changing configure.py from NonMatching to Matching for that object. The only comment is an automated GALE01 report showing linked-code progress and one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.

key_files.role: Added ItemStateTable it_803F9378 with motion 0 anim/phys/coll callbacks for the Master Hand laser item TU.
Changed the itmasterhandlaser object from NonMatching to Matching in MatchingFor.

what_changed.text: Added a global ItemStateTable named it_803F9378 containing one state entry with state id 0 and callbacks itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Anim, itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Phys, and itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Coll.
Updated configure.py so melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Automated report recorded one new match for main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser .data, increasing that unit's .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.

smart_moves.text: Kept the code change small and tied the configure.py matching flip to the missing item state table data for the same TU.

decomp_lessons.text: For an item TU that is otherwise ready to link, a missing ItemStateTable definition can be the final required data-side piece before changing the object from NonMatching to Matching. In this PR, adding it_803F9378 immediately accompanied the configure.py Matching flip and the bot reported the TU's .data as newly matched.

naming_conventions.text: The added item state table uses an address-style global name, it_803F9378, rather than a semantic name; the PR evidence does not justify a rename.
The callback names follow the existing itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Anim/Phys/Coll pattern visible in the added table.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Matching tactic was data completion plus configure classification: add the missing ItemStateTable and then mark the object Matching.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed linked code +0.05% (+1952 bytes), matched data +16 bytes, linked data +56 bytes, and one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.

follow_up_queries: item state table missing data matching flip

notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments.
The diff is small and direct, so changed files and added symbols are well supported.
The automated report supports matching impact, but the slice does not include build logs, objdiff details, or author explanation.
No reviewer intent or broader naming standard can be inferred from this PR alone.

accepted_candidate_records.title: itmasterhandlaser linked by adding its item state table
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2520 shows a focused item TU link: add ItemStateTable it_803F9378 with the Master Hand laser motion callbacks, then change melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The bot report recorded one new .data match for main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.

source_update_candidates.title: itmasterhandlaser item TU marked Matching in PR 2520
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2520 changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c is listed as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...), after adding ItemStateTable it_803F9378 in src/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as a path/status fact, but should be curated against current configure.py before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every item Matching flip should add an ItemStateTable; this PR only demonstrates that for itmasterhandlaser.
Do not rename it_803F9378 from this PR evidence; the diff keeps the address-style name and provides no semantic naming rationale.
Do not claim human review approval beyond merge status; the slice contains no human review comments.
